---
schema: verdi.deviation/v1
covers: 65d2bea679a3fb020f99c05be4ae621c7f8b8575
findings:
  - { id: judged-ac3-feature-spec-stale-fixture-gap, kind: judged, text: "ac-3 requires a one-defect fixture per defect class named in ac-1 at each scope, and ac-1's feature clause explicitly includes 'the same two' (spec-stale, pending-supersession) for features; closepreflightfeature_test.go covers only the feature-specific classes (outcome floor, unreconciled stub, open implementing story) plus ready-then-close — no feature-scope fixture ever constructs a spec-stale defect on a feature spec and proves --preflight's disclosure (incl. printSpecStalePathIfFailing's deviation-report.md path for the feature's own directory) agrees with a real feature close's refusal. The shared checkSpecStaleCondition is proven only at story scope, so the feature-scope agreement property for this class is aspirational, not checkable as ac-3 demands. (Feature-scope pending-supersession is structurally inexpressible — condition 5 passes vacuously per closuregatefeature.go's own disclosed reasoning — but spec-stale is fully expressible and simply untested.) (confidence 0.40)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Accepted-deviation per ADJ-56/ADJ-57's closing-clause valve (restates the original pf-judge-2 class): feature-scope spec-stale shares the identical checkSpecStaleCondition that story scope already exercises, so drift is structurally unlikely though not fixture-proven at feature scope; non-behavioral, filed for the Phase-5 test-honesty queue. This disposition is the store-resident record." }
  - { id: judged-ac3-unauthored-close-reason-not-asserted, kind: judged, text: "ac-3 (and closepreflight_test.go's own header) requires the real close on the byte-identical fixture to be asserted as 'refusing for exactly that same reason ... not merely that it also fails'; the 'unauthored attestation scaffold' subtest (closepreflight_test.go:433-465) asserts only runClose's exit code 1 with no assertion on close's stdout refusal reason (the eligibility FAIL line every sibling subtest checks), so for this defect class the agreement is proven only at exit-code granularity. (confidence 0.35)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Accepted-deviation per ADJ-64's closing clause (genuinely-new, confidence 0.35, non-behavioral): the unauthored-scaffold agreement subtest asserts close's exit-code-1 refusal but not its stdout FAIL reason at full granularity — a test-honesty gap in the same class ADJ-56/57's valve and ADJ-64's preflight test-honesty angle route to Phase 5; the shared evaluation functions make drift unlikely. Reported verbatim for merge review." }
  - { id: judged-feature-nosignal-ac-no-remedy, kind: judged, text: "A feature AC with no implementing story at all but an already-satisfied outcome floor folds to no-signal (featurefold.go:243-244) with Floor.Satisfied true: unmetFeatureACDetail prints nothing for it (renderFeatureFloorGap returns \"\"), condition 3 passes vacuously over the empty story set, and the only disclosure is the gate's coarse 'ac-X=no-signal' status token. ac-1's intent — every unmet AC names what is missing and how to remedy it — is not met for this shape: nothing tells the operator the missing input is an implements edge/implementing story rather than an evidence file, and no fixture exercises the shape. (confidence 0.30)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Accepted-deviation per ADJ-64's closing clause (genuinely-new, confidence 0.30): a feature AC with no implementing story but a satisfied outcome floor folds to no-signal and does not block closure (condition 3 passes vacuously), so it is informational, not an unmet blocking AC; whether it warrants a remedy hint beyond the no-signal status token is a marginal disclosure-completeness question filed for the Phase-5 quality angle. Reported verbatim for merge review." }
  - { id: judged-dc1-disclosure-skipped-on-operational-exit, kind: judged, text: "ac-1's dc-1 clause says the CI-publish-guard disclosure fires 'whenever preflight runs outside a detected CI environment without --force-local'; runPreflight (closepreflight.go:107) prints it only after the gate returns without error, so every exit-2 path (unresolvable ref, rev-parse failure, gate operational error) skips it. This matches the clause's intent (only a ready/unmet verdict can be mistaken for 'a real close will succeed'), but is a literal narrowing of 'whenever'. (confidence 0.15)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Accepted-deviation per ADJ-56/ADJ-57's closing-clause valve (restates the original pf-judge-5/pf-4 class): the CI-guard disclosure's omission on the exit-2 operational-error outcome matches dc-1's intent (no ready verdict to mistake for success), a literal narrowing of whenever the spec text does not itself state; non-behavioral, filed for the Phase-5 quality queue. This disposition is the store-resident record." }
digest: sha256:2edab55c17d189def1c91ac59bd6873c0e4602c8b6ef9b689ac65472b0043bcf
integrity: sha256:788685ac9093d085984b29322fc530ecab3b23d7faee3d8565c3e044e418795a
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"ac3-feature-spec-stale-fixture-gap\",\"text\":\"ac-3 requires a one-defect fixture per defect class named in ac-1 at each scope, and ac-1's feature clause explicitly includes 'the same two' (spec-stale, pending-supersession) for features; closepreflightfeature_test.go covers only the feature-specific classes (outcome floor, unreconciled stub, open implementing story) plus ready-then-close — no feature-scope fixture ever constructs a spec-stale defect on a feature spec and proves --preflight's disclosure (incl. printSpecStalePathIfFailing's deviation-report.md path for the feature's own directory) agrees with a real feature close's refusal. The shared checkSpecStaleCondition is proven only at story scope, so the feature-scope agreement property for this class is aspirational, not checkable as ac-3 demands. (Feature-scope pending-supersession is structurally inexpressible — condition 5 passes vacuously per closuregatefeature.go's own disclosed reasoning — but spec-stale is fully expressible and simply untested.)\",\"confidence\":0.4},{\"id\":\"ac3-unauthored-close-reason-not-asserted\",\"text\":\"ac-3 (and closepreflight_test.go's own header) requires the real close on the byte-identical fixture to be asserted as 'refusing for exactly that same reason ... not merely that it also fails'; the 'unauthored attestation scaffold' subtest (closepreflight_test.go:433-465) asserts only runClose's exit code 1 with no assertion on close's stdout refusal reason (the eligibility FAIL line every sibling subtest checks), so for this defect class the agreement is proven only at exit-code granularity.\",\"confidence\":0.35},{\"id\":\"feature-nosignal-ac-no-remedy\",\"text\":\"A feature AC with no implementing story at all but an already-satisfied outcome floor folds to no-signal (featurefold.go:243-244) with Floor.Satisfied true: unmetFeatureACDetail prints nothing for it (renderFeatureFloorGap returns \\\"\\\"), condition 3 passes vacuously over the empty story set, and the only disclosure is the gate's coarse 'ac-X=no-signal' status token. ac-1's intent — every unmet AC names what is missing and how to remedy it — is not met for this shape: nothing tells the operator the missing input is an implements edge/implementing story rather than an evidence file, and no fixture exercises the shape.\",\"confidence\":0.3},{\"id\":\"dc1-disclosure-skipped-on-operational-exit\",\"text\":\"ac-1's dc-1 clause says the CI-publish-guard disclosure fires 'whenever preflight runs outside a detected CI environment without --force-local'; runPreflight (closepreflight.go:107) prints it only after the gate returns without error, so every exit-2 path (unresolvable ref, rev-parse failure, gate operational error) skips it. This matches the clause's intent (only a ready/unmet verdict can be mistaken for 'a real close will succeed'), but is a literal narrowing of 'whenever'.\",\"confidence\":0.15}]}" }
frozen: { at: 2026-07-17, commit: 65d2bea679a3fb020f99c05be4ae621c7f8b8575 }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v0, inputs: [spec/close-preflight@65d2bea679a3fb020f99c05be4ae621c7f8b8575, spec/close-preflight@48d98b3a39de475e40b37255bc7ce257ccc21333], digest: sha256:2edab55c17d189def1c91ac59bd6873c0e4602c8b6ef9b689ac65472b0043bcf, integrity: sha256:788685ac9093d085984b29322fc530ecab3b23d7faee3d8565c3e044e418795a }
---
# Alignment report

## Computed

(none)

### Boundary diff vs acceptance baseline

(no impacted services)

### Diagram alignment

- (no accepted proposals)
- (no illustrative diagrams in this spec's body)

## Judged

- **judged-ac3-feature-spec-stale-fixture-gap** [accepted-deviation]: ac-3 requires a one-defect fixture per defect class named in ac-1 at each scope, and ac-1's feature clause explicitly includes 'the same two' (spec-stale, pending-supersession) for features; closepreflightfeature_test.go covers only the feature-specific classes (outcome floor, unreconciled stub, open implementing story) plus ready-then-close — no feature-scope fixture ever constructs a spec-stale defect on a feature spec and proves --preflight's disclosure (incl. printSpecStalePathIfFailing's deviation-report.md path for the feature's own directory) agrees with a real feature close's refusal. The shared checkSpecStaleCondition is proven only at story scope, so the feature-scope agreement property for this class is aspirational, not checkable as ac-3 demands. (Feature-scope pending-supersession is structurally inexpressible — condition 5 passes vacuously per closuregatefeature.go's own disclosed reasoning — but spec-stale is fully expressible and simply untested.) (confidence 0.40) — Accepted-deviation per ADJ-56/ADJ-57's closing-clause valve (restates the original pf-judge-2 class): feature-scope spec-stale shares the identical checkSpecStaleCondition that story scope already exercises, so drift is structurally unlikely though not fixture-proven at feature scope; non-behavioral, filed for the Phase-5 test-honesty queue. This disposition is the store-resident record.
- **judged-ac3-unauthored-close-reason-not-asserted** [accepted-deviation]: ac-3 (and closepreflight_test.go's own header) requires the real close on the byte-identical fixture to be asserted as 'refusing for exactly that same reason ... not merely that it also fails'; the 'unauthored attestation scaffold' subtest (closepreflight_test.go:433-465) asserts only runClose's exit code 1 with no assertion on close's stdout refusal reason (the eligibility FAIL line every sibling subtest checks), so for this defect class the agreement is proven only at exit-code granularity. (confidence 0.35) — Accepted-deviation per ADJ-64's closing clause (genuinely-new, confidence 0.35, non-behavioral): the unauthored-scaffold agreement subtest asserts close's exit-code-1 refusal but not its stdout FAIL reason at full granularity — a test-honesty gap in the same class ADJ-56/57's valve and ADJ-64's preflight test-honesty angle route to Phase 5; the shared evaluation functions make drift unlikely. Reported verbatim for merge review.
- **judged-feature-nosignal-ac-no-remedy** [accepted-deviation]: A feature AC with no implementing story at all but an already-satisfied outcome floor folds to no-signal (featurefold.go:243-244) with Floor.Satisfied true: unmetFeatureACDetail prints nothing for it (renderFeatureFloorGap returns ""), condition 3 passes vacuously over the empty story set, and the only disclosure is the gate's coarse 'ac-X=no-signal' status token. ac-1's intent — every unmet AC names what is missing and how to remedy it — is not met for this shape: nothing tells the operator the missing input is an implements edge/implementing story rather than an evidence file, and no fixture exercises the shape. (confidence 0.30) — Accepted-deviation per ADJ-64's closing clause (genuinely-new, confidence 0.30): a feature AC with no implementing story but a satisfied outcome floor folds to no-signal and does not block closure (condition 3 passes vacuously), so it is informational, not an unmet blocking AC; whether it warrants a remedy hint beyond the no-signal status token is a marginal disclosure-completeness question filed for the Phase-5 quality angle. Reported verbatim for merge review.
- **judged-dc1-disclosure-skipped-on-operational-exit** [accepted-deviation]: ac-1's dc-1 clause says the CI-publish-guard disclosure fires 'whenever preflight runs outside a detected CI environment without --force-local'; runPreflight (closepreflight.go:107) prints it only after the gate returns without error, so every exit-2 path (unresolvable ref, rev-parse failure, gate operational error) skips it. This matches the clause's intent (only a ready/unmet verdict can be mistaken for 'a real close will succeed'), but is a literal narrowing of 'whenever'. (confidence 0.15) — Accepted-deviation per ADJ-56/ADJ-57's closing-clause valve (restates the original pf-judge-5/pf-4 class): the CI-guard disclosure's omission on the exit-2 operational-error outcome matches dc-1's intent (no ready verdict to mistake for success), a literal narrowing of whenever the spec text does not itself state; non-behavioral, filed for the Phase-5 quality queue. This disposition is the store-resident record.
